schema_rule: The filename and its parent folder hold everything constant within a file (sample, pulse width, temperature, frequency, spacing); the array axes hold what varies (phase, time) together with the measured I/Q. The phase axis and the per-phase wall clock are stored inside the .npz.

columns.name: temperature_mK
columns.computed_from: parent folder 'npz_data_base_{T}mK' and the filename's exp_ID
columns.description: Fridge set point — the primary variable of this experiment. This is a SET POINT, not a measured temperature: these files carry no thermometry arrays (experiment 11's do). Each temperature is a separate run, thermalised >1 hour beforehand.

columns.name: frequency_MHz
columns.description: Drive and readout frequency. Both pulses fire at this frequency.

columns.name: spacing_ns
columns.description: Gap between the end of the first pulse and the start of the second. Stored in nanoseconds rather than the filenames' microseconds so the values are exact in float32. Only

columns.name: phase_deg
columns.description: Phase of the FIRST pulse. The second pulse's phase is held fixed. 0 and 360 deg are the same physical point, acquired independently.

columns.name: timestamp_ns
columns.description: Time within the recorded trace. Directly comparable across spacings and temperatures because the second pulse is anchored.

columns.name: elapsed_s
columns.computed_from: time_stamp_list in each .npz, minus the campaign start
columns.description: Wall-clock seconds since the campaign started (the 8 mK run), recorded once per phase point. The campaign spans about three days, so this also encodes which run a row belongs to and the thermalisation gaps between set points.

columns.name: I
columns.description: Raw in-phase homodyne signal, averaged over shots.

columns.name: Q
columns.description: Raw quadrature homodyne signal, averaged over shots. Combined with I to compute magnitude and phase.
